ICYMI

FOLLOWING THE ISRAELI STRIKE ON BEIRUT’S DAHIEH DISTRICT, REPORTS INDICATE THAT IRAN’S SUPREME NATIONAL SECURITY COUNCIL (SNSC) HAS CONVENED AN EMERGENCY SESSION.

- ACCORDING TO IRANIAN-LINKED SOURCES, THE MEETING IS BEING HELD IN A LIMITED FORMAT BECAUSE PRE-PLANNED INSTRUCTIONS HAD ALREADY BEEN ISSUED. THOSE INSTRUCTIONS ARE REPORTEDLY BASED ON THE PRINCIPLE OF THE “UNITY OF THE RESISTANCE FRONTS” REPEATEDLY EMPHASIZED BY SUPREME LEADER MOJTABA KHAMENEI.

- THE TIMING IS NOTEWORTHY. ISRAELI MEDIA REPORT THAT THE STRIKE TARGETED A HEZBOLLAH COMMAND FACILITY, POSSIBLY EMPTY AT THE TIME OF THE ATTACK, AND THAT ISRAELI OFFICIALS ASSESSED IRAN WOULD NOT RESPOND DIRECTLY. AT THE SAME TIME, HEZBOLLAH REPORTEDLY LAUNCHED A SURFACE-TO-AIR MISSILE AT AN ISRAELI AIRCRAFT OVER SOUTHERN LEBANON.

- ALSO SIGNIFICANT ARE PRESIDENT TRUMP’S LATEST REMARKS. TRUMP STATED THAT LEBANON DOES NOT NEED TO BE PART OF THE CEASEFIRE WITH IRAN AND RULED OUT BOTH SANCTIONS RELIEF AND THE UNFREEZING OF IRANIAN FUNDS. TAKEN TOGETHER, THESE COMMENTS APPEAR TO SIGNAL A TOUGHER U.S. APPROACH TOWARD TEHRAN AND MAY FURTHER COMPLICATE AN ALREADY FRAGILE DIPLOMATIC PROCESS, RAISING QUESTIONS ABOUT THE FUTURE OF THE TALKS.

- THE FOCUS NOW SHIFTS TO TEHRAN AND TO WHETHER THE SNSC MEETING RESULTS IN A COORDINATED RESPONSE THROUGH THE WIDER AXIS OF RESISTANCE RATHER THAN A DIRECT IRANIAN REACTION.
